Badger Grapple Red — v20.0 Campaign Restoration

WHAT CHANGED (systems only — zero new art, zero new map coordinates,
zero changes to any painted background, existing NPC placement/dialogue
preserved verbatim except two NPCs now trigger real battles):

1. ROSTER: 8 -> 23 wrestlers. All 8 original entries kept at their exact
   original stats/moves (no rebalancing of what already existed). Added:
   evolution chains for the 3 starters and 3 commons (evolve at Lv10-14
   depending on line), 2 new dedicated gym-boss entries (The Funk Doctor
   for Scramble, The Anchor for Top), plus wild-variety recruits. All new
   entries reuse the 5 existing battle sprite sheets via tint, same as
   the originals — no new art required.

2. MOVES: 15 -> 26, filling out every style with a cheap-accurate,
   mid-power, and high-risk option.

3. REAL BADGE LADDER: the Championship gym leader was a second copy of
   Captain Neutral awarding the same badge (a no-op fight). It's now a
   distinct Top-style boss ("The Anchor") awarding a real Top Badge.
   River Trail gained its own gym leader ("The Funk Doctor", Scramble
   style) at a mathematically-verified-safe, previously-empty tile
   (24,9) — confirmed open and non-grass against the exact isBlocked/
   isGrass formulas already in the code, so no collision risk. The
   River -> Championship corridor is now gated: it requires both the
   Neutral and Scramble badges, so the three gyms form a real sequence
   (Conference -> River -> Championship) instead of three unconnected
   fights.

4. MULTI-WRESTLER BATTLES: gym leaders and trainers now field real
   2-3 wrestler teams (BattleScene supports enemy teams; on KO it
   announces the next wrestler and continues). Previously every fight,
   including "gym leaders," was a single wrestler.

5. TRAINER BATTLES: two NPCs on Campus Quad already had challenge
   dialogue that did nothing ("Buckshot: Want to test yourself?" just
   triggered a random wild encounter; the Rival NPC only showed flavor
   text). Both now trigger real one-time trainer battles with their own
   teams and Grit/Rep rewards, using their EXISTING coordinates — no new
   interaction zones were added. Defeated trainers persist (state.
   trainersDefeated) and show a "beaten" line on re-approach instead of
   refighting.

6. FAINT HANDLING: previously any single loss (lead wrestler's HP hits
   0) ended the whole battle as a loss, even with 5 healthy wrestlers
   still in the box. Now: if any party member is still healthy, you're
   forced into the WRESTLER menu to send in the next one (free, no
   extra hit taken) and the fight continues. Only a fully-wiped party
   is a loss. Voluntary mid-battle swaps (not forced by a faint) now
   correctly cost your turn, matching genre convention.

7. MID-BATTLE RECRUITING: the BAG "RECRUIT FLYER" item was a no-op
   ("Save flyers for recruiting."). It now actually attempts a catch
   on the current wild wrestler, with odds that improve as you weaken
   them — the classic "weaken then catch" loop. Pre-battle scouting
   (Scout Report screen) and the post-win invite screen both still
   work exactly as before; this adds a third option rather than
   replacing anything.

8. SAVES: added state.trainersDefeated (defaults to {}, merges
   cleanly for existing saves — normalizeState migrates old saves
   with no data loss).

WHAT DELIBERATELY DIDN'T CHANGE: painted area art, tile/sprite assets,
collision zones (isBlocked/spotKind) for Field House/Campus Quad/Study
Hall/Lakeshore/Downtown, the opening objective loop and its dialogue,
NPC patrol routes, hidden items, the day/period practice system, save
key, UI look (240x170 canvas, same fonts/box styles).

VERIFIED BEFORE SHIPPING:
- tools/validate.mjs: every move/roster/area/trainer cross-reference
  checked programmatically (all valid).
- tools/balance_sim.mjs: Monte Carlo combat sim (300 trials/scenario)
  using the exact damage formula from BattleScene. Results: Conference
  gym is a real wall below Lv9 (~10-25% win rate) and clears reliably
  right around the first evolution at Lv10 (~83%) — intentional, mirrors
  genre pacing. River and Championship both require a real party (a
  solo wrestler underperforms a 2-3 wrestler team by 20-40 points of
  win rate at the same total level budget), which was the actual goal
  of restoring multi-wrestler teams and faint-based swapping.
- node --check on every modified file.

Cache: ?v=200. Proof: title screen reads "CAMPAIGN v20.0".
